CheckTitle: Conditional Access policies enforce MFA for device registration and Intune enrollment
Description: Conditional Access policies requiring **multifactor authentication** for device registration and Intune enrollment prevent unauthorized users from registering rogue devices or enrolling into device management with stolen credentials.
Risk:
Without MFA for device registration, an attacker with stolen credentials could register rogue devices into the directory, gaining persistent access and bypassing compliance-based Conditional Access policies.

Without MFA for Intune enrollment, compromised credentials may be used to enroll unauthorized devices, enabling lateral movement and data exfiltration.

Code.Other:
1. Navigate to the Microsoft Entra admin center (https://entra.microsoft.com).
2. Expand **Protection** > **Conditional Access** and select **Policies**.
3. Create a policy for device registration:
   - Under **Users**, include **All users**.
   - Under **Target resources**, select **User actions** and check **Register or join devices**.
   - Under **Grant**, select **Require multifactor authentication**.
4. Create a policy for Intune enrollment:
   - Under **Users**, include **All users**.
   - Under **Target resources**, select **Cloud apps** and add **Microsoft Intune Enrollment**.
   - Under **Grant**, select **Require multifactor authentication**.
   - Under **Session**, enable **Sign-in frequency** and set to **Every time**.
5. Set both policies to **Report-only** until validated, then enable them.

Recommendation.Text: Enforce MFA for both device registration and Intune enrollment through Conditional Access policies. Set sign-in frequency to **Every time** for Intune enrollment to prevent session reuse during device enrollment operations.

Notes: Requires Entra ID P1 or later license.
